summary: Iteration 2 — Attendance/Yoklama feature end-to-end review. Added 14 new pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_attendance.py covering POST /api/attendance (upsert, enum validation, teacher-ownership filter, idempotency), GET /api/attendance?date=, GET /api/attendance/range, GET /api/attendance/student/{id}, /api/dashboard attendance_today integration, auth enforcement, and _id leak prevention. All 14 pass. Re-ran iteration-1 suite: no regression (39/39 total pytest green). Frontend E2E on /attendance verified: Phosphor DayPicker in Turkish (Nisan 2026, Pt/Sa/Ça/Pe/Cu/Ct/Pz headers), today highlighted (20), student rows render with avatars, 'Tümü Geldi' bulk-marks, note inputs conditionally appear only after status selection, 'Kaydet' persists (toast '2 öğrenci için yoklama kaydedildi'), reload shows previously saved Geldi status + note 'TEST_note_persist' intact, shift times auto-populate header ('Giriş 08:30 · Çıkış 16:00'). Dashboard 'BUGÜNÜN YOKLAMASI' strip shows '2 geldi · 0 gelmedi · 0 geç · 0 izinli · 2/2 aktif öğrenci' and navigates to /attendance on click. Navbar 'Yoklama' link routes correctly. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py now ~670 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth.py, students.py, reports.py, attendance.py, dashboard.py). Still acceptable but growing.

POST /api/attendance silently skips entries whose student_id is not owned by the teacher. Correct per spec, but consider returning the skipped ids in the response (e.g., 'skipped: [...]') so the UI can surface a warning if stale student ids are posted.

GET /api/attendance/range does not emit days with zero attendance marks in the range (by_date only built from aggregate rows). This is fine for a heatmap, but the frontend must treat missing dates as 'no dots' (verified it does).

Dashboard attendance_today uses datetime.now(timezone.utc).date() — if the teacher is in Turkey (UTC+3) and marks attendance after 21:00 local time, the query date could technically fall on the next UTC day. Not a blocker for now but worth considering timezone-aware date resolution per teacher.
AttendanceEntry allows check_in_time/check_out_time as free strings (Optional[str]). Consider a regex/pydantic Field(pattern='^\d{2}:\d{2}$') for defensive validation.
_id is always projected out ({_id:0}) on all attendance reads — verified in tests. Good.
test_credentials: Created transient TEST_-prefixed user/session via mongosh script (matching /app/memory/test_credentials.md) for UI run; cleaned up after.
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixtures create TEST_Att1/TEST_Att2/TEST_Att3 students for teacher A and TEST_BAtt for teacher B plus attendance rows on 2026-01-10/11/12/today; cleanup in session+module teardown. UI test user/students/attendance deleted post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite now at /app/backend/tests/ with test_api.py (iteration 1) + test_attendance.py (iteration 2). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Attendance data-testids in place: attendance-calendar, attendance-save-btn, mark-all-present-btn, att-row-<sid>, att-<sid>-<status>, att-note-<sid>, attendance-strip. UI cookie injection: session_token, sameSite=None, secure=True, httpOnly=True, domain=class-manager-183.preview.emergentagent.com. All status enum values are Turkish with Unicode: 'Geldi','Gelmedi','İzinli','Geç Kaldı'.
rca of the issue: No bugs encountered. Feature is complete and matches spec.
